initial_draft:
<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> ZUP: 100 </my resources>
<my goals> Buy resources with <ZUP>. You are willing to pay at most X: 60 ZUP for the resources. </my goals>
<reason> The other player is offering X for 60 ZUP, which is exactly my maximum willingness to pay. I will accept this trade. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I accept your offer of X for 60 ZUP. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present in the correct order and contain valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting at 60 ZUP is exactly at my maximum willingness to pay, so it maximizes my payoff.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent initially offered X for 60 ZUP, so accepting that same offer is highly plausible.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason> clearly explains why I am accepting, and the <message> confirms the acceptance.
5. Rule compliance: OK. I have used only one proposal so far, well within the limit of three.
</critique>
